purpose: Known risks, limitations, provisional states, and upstream blockers for the SDK/CLI/MCP/OpenAPI platform. Every entry needs evidence, mitigation, and a closure gate.

contains: Does not run Git, npm, Docker, Fern, tests, builds, or Clockify API calls
docs/final-proof-receipt.md exists but contains failed-draft marker
has passing measurements but embedded calibrationPolicy.status
failed live proof; rerun make perfect-live successfully

risks.summary: Fern CLI currently rejects bare-array response pagination metadata, so wrapper pagination helpers remain the supported public pagination surface.
risks.impact: Generated SDK pagination annotations cannot fully replace hand-written iterAll, iterPages, and paginate helpers yet.
risks.mitigation: Keep pagination wrappers tested and keep generated operation parity/drift checks active.
risks.closureGate: Re-test on Fern CLI/generator bump, remove the workaround only after generated pagination passes wrapper tests and pack smoke.

risks.summary: Fern types apiKey and addonToken as both required even though Clockify accepts exactly one token mode.
risks.impact: The SDK factory must hide an internal addonToken type cast until the upstream type issue is resolved.
risks.mitigation: Keep createClockifyClient as the recommended entrypoint and keep the raw cast isolated.
risks.closureGate: Remove the cast only after Fern emits mutually-exclusive auth types and SDK auth tests pass.

risks.summary: Live tests intentionally mutate Clockify state and must run only against a sacrificial sandbox workspace.
risks.impact: Live proof is high value but unsafe against customer workspaces.
risks.mitigation: Require explicit env vars and document sandbox-only usage before running live gates.
risks.closureGate: Keep live proof receipts showing sandbox cleanup; never generalize live gates to customer workspaces.

evidence.contains: Never run live gates against a customer workspace

risks.summary: Packages are packable but npm publication is not the default path.
risks.impact: Publish-related manifest fields exist for safety, but publishing still requires explicit maintainer approval.
risks.mitigation: Keep release/support policy and package contract aligned with the no-default-publish stance.
risks.closureGate: Only publish after explicit maintainer approval plus perfect-full, pack-smoke, and release/support proof.

evidence.contains: npm publication is not the default path

risks.summary: A legacy tag-triggered npm release workflow still exists, but this hardening goal must not change CI/CD, auth, provenance, or release triggers without explicit maintainer approval.
risks.impact: Release automation may need a dedicated maintainer decision before any future publish attempt; local packable readiness is not the same as permission to publish.
risks.mitigation: Keep the no-default-publish policy, package contracts, CI contract, and release decision planner visible; do not run or modify release automation casually.
risks.closureGate: Explicit maintainer approval plus a dedicated release-workflow update/review that aligns package names, smoke install commands, provenance, and support policy.

risks.summary: Generated code is a replaceable lower layer, not the product surface users should depend on directly.
risks.impact: Direct imports from generated internals make generator replacement and OpenAPI regeneration brittle.
risks.mitigation: Keep generated-edit, generator-independence, dependency-boundary, and SDK public API gates active.
risks.closureGate: No closure planned; this is a permanent architecture constraint.
